name: Exim mail log
pattern: ^(?<timestamp>\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\s+\d{2}:\d{2}:\d{2}(?:\s+[+\-]\d{4})?)\s+(?:(?<pid>\[\d+\])\s+)?(?<message_id>[A-Za-z0-9]{6}-[A-Za-z0-9]{6}-[A-Za-z0-9]{2,4})?\s*(?<flag>(?:<=|=>|->|>>|\*\*|==|<>|Completed|SMTP connection from|SMTP connection outbound|no host name found for IP address|no IP address found for host|TLS error|Start queue run|End queue run|cancelled by timeout|temporarily refused connection|H=|U=|F=))?\s*(?<message>.*)$
sampleLines: 2024-04-28 04:02:04 1qK3dV-0001Bp-7y => recipient@example.org R=dnslookup T=remote_smtp H=mx.example.org [203.0.113.1] X=TLS1.3:ECDHE_RSA_AES_256_GCM_SHA384:256 CV=yes
2024-04-28 04:02:06 +0000 [12345] H=(badmailer) [198.51.100.5] sender verify defer for <bad@example.com>: temporary problem
description: Adapted from logstash-plugins/logstash-patterns-core patterns/ecs-v1/exim (Apache-2.0), entry EXIM_MAINLOG. Inlined %{EXIM_DATE}, %{EXIM_MSGID}, %{EXIM_FLAGS}, %{NOTSPACE}, %{GREEDYDATA} macros to plain PCRE2; the flag tokens are the message-arrival ('<='), delivery ('=>'), additional-address ('->'), local-bounce ('**'), defer ('=='), and frozen ('<>') indicators from Exim's mainlog (see Exim spec chapter 52 'Log Files').
